engineering_rigor_preservation.rationale: The current engineering constraints make the system reproducible; the fix is to add design/aesthetic taxonomy above them, not to weaken traceability or release gates.

taxonomy_bias_summary.required_correction: Add a design_motif_layer that carries motif_id, motif_family, layout_recipe, typography_treatment, style_family, and motif_fidelity_checks while preserving engineering gates.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Tracks source availability, source type, and decomposition gaps explicitly.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Inventory fields prove coverage but do not yet encode motif, spatial recipe, or style family.
evidence: run2_73_source_quality_audit records source coverage and decomposition status.
Several records remain source inventory until shot/layout-level signals are decomposed.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: Renderer can cite sources but cannot reproduce the visual method behind those sources.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep source availability, anti-copy boundaries, and decomposition gap labels.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Forces reproducible stage boundaries, negative controls, public blocking, and trace gates.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Gate names describe whether a step is allowed, not what aesthetic move must survive the step.
evidence: skill_workflow.json and run2_8_workflow_gate_matrix.json protect execution order.
The same gate vocabulary can dominate prompt and renderer instructions.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: Slides inherit status/gate language and can read as process documentation.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep the stage gates because they make the product reproducible and auditable.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Maps tutorial rules to structured renderer actions with traceability.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Rules are action-safe but not always motif-specific enough to recreate a tutorial look.
evidence: run2_73_tutorial_to_design_moves maps hierarchy, whitespace, rhythm, and motion rules.
Renderer actions emphasize target selectors and fallbacks more than visual motif fidelity.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: The system applies rules like fewer labels, but the page may still look generic.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep traceable action mapping from tutorial rule to renderer action.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Defines reusable page modules such as hero_field, before_after_theater, and product_reveal.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Module IDs are stronger than rectangles, but they still need motif recipes and style parameters.
evidence: run2_73_visual_grammar_modules contains public-facing module choices.
Later reruns still collapse many modules into similar fields and panels.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: Pages differ in intent but remain visually similar because motif execution is under-specified.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep role-to-module mapping and page differentiation constraints.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Converts scene plans into renderer-safe containers, bindings, and execution guards.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Adapter terms can flatten design intent into proof_panel, hero_object, and status-safe primitives.
evidence: run2_73_renderer_adapter_contracts prevents renderer/public release leakage.
It does not guarantee that visual motifs survive translation into native shapes.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: Outputs become valid native-PPT structures but can read as wireframe diagrams.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep execution guards and native editable output constraints.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Binds headline, proof, supporting copy, and notes to source IDs and visual objects.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Socket binding controls where text is allowed, but not enough about editorial typography treatment.
evidence: run2_73_text_binding_strategy routes traceability off canvas.
The stronger O1 text plan still needs a motif-level typography treatment to avoid dead layouts.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: Text is safer and denser, but blocks can feel stiff and disconnected from composition.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep canvas/note routing and source-bound copy units.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Introduces headline, subhead, proof sentence, caption, capacity, and label bans.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: The plan improves readability but still does not select style-specific text treatments.
evidence: run2_81_text_composition_typography_plan bans floating labels and traceability on canvas.
Run 2.82 can still feel text-heavy without richer motif/style routing.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: Pages gain text hierarchy but may lose the earlier modular, matrix, and overlay visual energy.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep readable hierarchy, object anchors, and note routes.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Produces four arms and records consumed sources, outputs, viewer updates, and checks.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Rerun success proves generation and viewer availability, not public-grade aesthetic fidelity.
evidence: run2_82_renderer_product_surface_text_composition_rerun_result records generated arms.
Quality verdict is explicitly deferred and public_ready remains false.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: The system may improve content and structure while still missing the desired presentation feel.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep four-arm comparisons and bad-control arms.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Repeated evaluations correctly block public release when wireframe or label issues remain.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Evaluation detects failures after the fact but does not itself teach renderer motif recipes.
evidence: run2_74, run2_76, run2_78, and run2_80 evaluations keep public_ready false.
Repeated blockers include wireframe, sparse text, labels, and absent product surface.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: The loop prevents false release, but can produce repeated repair cycles with diminishing visual gains.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep independent visual evaluation and public blocking.

layer_bias_records.engineering_strength: Make every data, workflow, renderer, and release boundary reproducible in CI-like checks.
layer_bias_records.design_signal_loss: Tests currently validate fields and gates more readily than motif fidelity and style fit.
evidence: validate_ppt_case_pack.py requires each run2 artifact and rejects public release leakage.
Motif fidelity checks are not yet first-class required fields.
layer_bias_records.impact_on_ppt: The project gets safer but can optimize toward passing schemas instead of design quality.
layer_bias_records.must_preserve: Keep validators and add design motif checks beside them.

run2_series_pattern.effect_on_ppt: The deck became more reproducible, but visual decomposition was still shallow.
More information entered the system, but product surfaces stayed schematic.

run2_series_pattern.dominant_work: Typography, readability, socket, and renderer fit gates.
run2_series_pattern.effect_on_ppt: Text became safer, but design moves were often reduced to capacity and placement rules.

run2_series_pattern.dominant_work: Validated A-F contracts, renderer repairs, O1 text composition, and O2 rerun.
run2_series_pattern.effect_on_ppt: Engineering confidence improved while motif fidelity and scenario style remain under-modeled.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Names the concrete design move extracted from a tutorial or case instead of only naming a gate.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Renderer must preserve the named motif across layout, text, and object drawing.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Groups related tutorial moves such as matrix, product theater, editorial spread, overlay, or sticker stack.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Style router can select compatible modules by family instead of generic rectangles.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Captures spatial composition as a recipe, not a vague module name.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Renderer receives explicit zones, focal object scale, overlaps, and reading path.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Describes how text, object, evidence, and background relate on the canvas.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Text cannot float because it must attach to an object, edge, rail, field, or panel relation.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Preserves the tutorial's headline, paragraph, caption, and label behavior.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Renderer applies type hierarchy as visual composition rather than only max_words.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Separates sparse premium pages, heavy editorial pages, and modular proof pages.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Renderer chooses density per scenario instead of flattening every slide to the same block system.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Keeps commercial style direction distinct from engineering status labels.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Color, depth, shape language, and asset treatment are routed by style family.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Matches usecase needs such as product pitch, teaching, finance, or technical proof.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Motifs can be constrained by scenario so decorative moves do not override business clarity.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Turns design motif into executable native-PPT drawing instructions.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Renderer can reproduce tutorial-derived visual effects without copying source assets.

missing_design_taxonomy.why_needed: Checks whether the rendered slide still looks like the intended motif.
missing_design_taxonomy.renderer_contract_implication: Evaluation can reject rectangle-only or wireframe outputs even if traceability passes.

required_next_layer.goal: Recover tutorial-derived modular, matrix, overlay, sticker-stack, product-theater, and editorial text motifs while keeping source trace and public-release gates.
